sourceMovie.title: Black Swan
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Black Swan works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Rosemary's Baby
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Black Swan clicked for you, Rosemary's Baby (1968) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Black Swan?
faq.answer: Try Rosemary's Baby, Stoker, Repulsion—each shares DNA with Black Swan while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Black Swan?
faq.answer: Black Swan (2010) is best described as Drama, Thriller, Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Black Swan worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
